c74object.name: dk.plotter
c74object.description: The dk.plotter object is a utility for visualizing corpora and trained classes.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithms have a look at these articles:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/umap
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/pca

outlet.description: Selected point from incoming matching or UI interaction.
Selected label output from incoming matching or UI interaction.

method.name: bgcolor
method.digest: Sets the background color of the plotter
method.description: Sets the background color of the plotter.

method.name: clear
method.digest: Clears the graph and all associated data and settings
method.description: Clears the graph and all associated data and settings.

method.name: pointcolor
method.description: The dk.plotter object is a utility for visualizing corpora and trained classes.

method.name: pointsize
method.description: The dk.plotter object is a utility for visualizing corpora and trained classes.

method.name: pointsizescale
method.description: The dk.plotter object is a utility for visualizing corpora and trained classes.

method.name: setpoint
method.description: The dk.plotter object is a utility for visualizing corpora and trained classes.

method.name: xrange
method.description: The dk.plotter object is a utility for visualizing corpora and trained classes.

method.name: yrange
method.description: The dk.plotter object is a utility for visualizing corpora and trained classes.

attribute.name: algorithm
attribute.description: Sets the dimensionality reduction algorithm (UMAP or PCA).

attribute.name: color
attribute.digest: Sets the color mapping for 3d and 4d projections
attribute.description: Set the color mapping for the 3d and 4d projections. The available options are: linear = Black - Blue - Green - Orange - Yellow map. The most perceptually accurate mapping. rainbow = Vivid rainbow colour map from blue to red with a small perceptual blind spot at yellow. Beware, the vivid colours may make you segment regions in your data on the basis of the colours in the map rather than the structure in your data. cyclic = Attractive six-colour cyclic with primaries and secondaries matched in lightness. greyscale = Standard grey scale. colorblind = Linear map with maximal chroma for Protanopic/Deuteranopic viewers. hsl = Even distribution based on Hue, Lightness, and Saturation. Offers wide visual contrast at the cost of distance accuracy in the data. As a rule of thumb linear offers the most accurate display and hsl offers the most visual contrast.

attribute.name: iterations
attribute.digest: The number of iterations that the algorithm will go through
attribute.description: The number of iterations that the algorithm will go through to optimise the new representation (minimum 1).

attribute.name: learnrate
attribute.description: The learning rate of the algorithm, aka how much of the error it uses to estimate the next iteration.

attribute.name: mindist
attribute.digest: Minimum distance each point is allowed to be from the others in the low dimension space
attribute.description: The minimum distance each point is allowed to be from the others in the low dimension space. Low values will make tighter clumps, and higher will spread the points more.

attribute.name: numneighbors
attribute.digest: Number of neighbors considered by the algorithm to balance local vs global structures
attribute.description: The number of neighbors considered by the algorithm to balance local vs global structures to conserve. Low values will prioritise preservation of the local structure while high values will prioritise preservation of the global structure.

attribute.name: size
attribute.description: Display size of UI (small: 169, medium: 235, large: 350, xl: 600).

attribute.name: whiten
attribute.digest: Whether or not to perform "whitening" on the output of PCA
attribute.description: A flag (0 or 1) indicating whether or not to perform "whitening" on the output of PCA.
